top_strategic.title: Longitudinal tracking possible: 54 players with 5+ clips
top_strategic.evidence: 54 players have enough data for improvement tracking. Top: PickleBill(423), Tmf(132), Chintan(101), Daniella(80), Dave (68)
top_strategic.action: Build player improvement trajectory product — track skill changes over time

top_strategic.title: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection
top_strategic.evidence: The camera sees these items but we detect no brands. ZERO detections: accessories, apparel, footwear, wristband. Near-zero: sunglasses(2). Each gap = a brand that could be paying for visibility data.
top_strategic.action: Add shoe/apparel/sunglasses brand detection examples to Gemini prompt — immediate revenue unlock

top_strategic.title: Endorsement intelligence: 141 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(665x), Tmf-JOOLA(285x), Daniella-JOOLA(178x), Adam-JOOLA(146x), Chintan-JOOLA(142x)
top_strategic.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

top_strategic.title: Social caption generation at 99.9% — ready to productize
top_strategic.evidence: Social media captions are generated for 4280/4283 clips. This is already a viable content-as-a-service product.
top_strategic.action: Create API endpoint for social caption generation — charge per clip

top_strategic.title: Production reality: 14/16 clips are 'amateur' — not broadcast
top_strategic.evidence: Gemini's visual assessment of production level across 16 clips: amateur=14, pro=1, semi-pro=1
top_strategic.action: Brand pitches should price the actual production tier buyers are seeing, not the aspirational one.

top_data.title: Viral shot: 'overhead' clips average 5.12 viral vs 'none' at 1
top_data.evidence: Shot viral averages: overhead=5.12, speed_up=4.27, reset=3.87, volley=3.52, dink=2.92, drive=2.87, serve=2.18, unknown=1.14, none=1
top_data.action: Prioritize highlight reels featuring high-viral shot types

top_data.title: Prompt fix: 5 badges consistently missed — add positive examples
top_data.evidence: Production awards these but DaaS misses them: 'rally master' (6 missed), 'new look' (5 missed), 'steady eddie' (4 missed), 'paddle tap celebration' (3 missed), 'fresh fit' (3 missed). These are real badges that our prompt doesn't detect.
top_data.action:
Add positive examples to Gemini prompt:
'When analyzing badges, make sure to check for these commonly missed badges:
  - rally master: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - new look: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - steady eddie: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - paddle tap celebration: Look for [describe visual pattern]
  - fresh fit: Look for [describe visual pattern]

top_data.title: DaaS badge prediction: F1=0.98 (precision=0.99, recall=0.97)
top_data.evidence: Across 370 verified clips: 1422 correct predictions, 16 false positives, 41 missed badges. Overall F1=0.980.
top_data.action: Target recall improvement: add more badge examples to Gemini prompt

top_data.title: Signature moves = viral gold: 4253 clips with signatures avg 2.9 viral vs 3.9
top_data.evidence: 4253 clips have identified signature moves, averaging 2.9 viral vs 3.9 for clips without.
top_data.action: Auto-tag signature move clips as premium content

top_data.title: Net/venue branding inflates 7 brands — true share-of-court differs from raw counts
top_data.evidence: Brands with significant venue-only detections (not product use): JOOLA (551/6941 are venue), Nicol (6/14 are venue), PUSHIT (5/9 are venue), Onix (4/45 are venue), adidas (4/47 are venue). Raw counts overstate market share. Product-use only: JOOLA=6390, Nike=683, PICKLEBALL=235, Reolink=132, HEAD=120.
top_data.action: Report TRUE share-of-court (product-use only) to brand partners, not raw detection counts

all_findings.title: Viral formula: quality (7.39 vs 4.46) + watchability (6.78 vs 2.47)
all_findings.evidence: Top 100 viral clips: quality=7.39, watchability=6.78. Bottom 100: quality=4.46, watchability=2.47. Both metrics track strongly with viral potential.
all_findings.action: Use (quality + watchability) / 2 as quick viral prediction score

all_findings.title: Endorsement intelligence: 141 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
all_findings.evidence: Top pairs: PickleBill-JOOLA(665x), Tmf-JOOLA(285x), Daniella-JOOLA(178x), Adam-JOOLA(146x), Chintan-JOOLA(142x)
all_findings.action: Build 'brand ambassador identification' product for equipment companies

all_findings.title: Video reveals 57 patterns metadata never saw
all_findings.evidence: Across 16 clips, Gemini video analysis surfaced 57 visual observations flagged as novel vs the text metadata. Sample: The footage is shot from a slightly elevated angle, looking down at the court, providing a clear view of playe; During Player 2's backhand counter-drop, their body is contorted in a lunging position, demonstrating athletic; The camera work is stable, suggesting a tripod or a well-stabilized handheld shot, with no noticeable cuts or
all_findings.action: Feed these patterns back into the metadata extraction prompt — they represent systematic blind spots in text-only analysis.

all_findings.title: Metadata quality drifts from video reality by 0.9 pts on average
all_findings.evidence: Across 16 clips, Gemini video-based quality ratings differ from metadata by avg -0.9. 6 clips have metadata OVERSTATING quality (>=2pt lie); 1 UNDERSTATING.
all_findings.action: Re-train or re-prompt the metadata quality signal — it's drifting from what humans actually see on screen.

all_findings.title: Rally length matters: long rallies are more viral (long=3.1 vs short=2.2)
all_findings.evidence: Avg rally: 15.6 shots. Long rallies (15+): 2181 clips, avg viral 3.1. Short (4-): 65 clips, avg viral 2.2.
all_findings.action: Use rally length as a quality signal in highlight curation

all_findings.title: Prompt fix: JOOLA appears in 253/370 verified clips — add disambiguation
all_findings.evidence: JOOLA dominates 68% of clips. Prompt should distinguish: paddle logo (real JOOLA use), net logo (JOOLA sponsors nets), and venue branding (JOOLA sponsors the facility).
all_findings.action:
Add to brand detection prompt:
'For JOOLA specifically, classify detection source:
  - paddle: JOOLA logo on paddle face = confirmed product use
  - net: JOOLA logo on net = venue sponsorship, NOT product use
  - venue: JOOLA on walls/banners = facility sponsorship
Only count paddle detections as brand_category: paddle'

all_findings.title: Viral arc: 'athletic' is 220.0x more likely in top clips (22.0% vs 0.0%)
all_findings.evidence: Story arc 'athletic' appears in 22.0% of top-viral clips but only 0.0% of bottom-viral clips.
all_findings.action: Prioritize 'athletic' clips in automated highlight reels

all_findings.title: Brand-skill split: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM users excel at kitchen (7.5), PSA users at power (6.29)
all_findings.evidence: Among brands with 10+ clips: PICKLEBALL KINGDOM has highest avg kitchen mastery (7.5), while PSA has highest power rating (6.29).
all_findings.action: Build brand-skill correlation report for brand partnerships

all_findings.title: Body language signals present in 5/16 clips — untapped signal
all_findings.evidence: Gemini video flagged body-language cues (celebrations, frustration, gestures) in 5 clips that metadata scores don't capture. These correlate with viral potential beyond shot mechanics.
all_findings.action: Add a 'body_language' detection pass to the extraction pipeline — these signals predict shareability.

all_findings.action: Brand pitches should price the actual production tier buyers are seeing, not the aspirational one.

all_findings.title: Skill standout: Ajr averages 8.5 in consistency across 6 clips
all_findings.evidence: Ajr skill profile: consistency=8.5, kitchen=8.2, touch=8.0, composure=7.5, court_iq=7.0, court_coverage=6.2, athleticism=5.2, power=3.0, creativity=3.0
all_findings.action: Feature Ajr in coaching product showcase

product_concept.name: BadgeBoost: Equipment Sponsorship Gap Filler
product_concept.first_screen: Dashboard showing 4 unsponsored equipment categories (paddles, shoes, bags, grips) with side-by-side comparison: current detection rate (0%) vs. potential with improved labeling (+47% estimated). Users see a 'Start Training' button to contribute verified examples for each category.
product_concept.why_powerful: Directly monetizes detection blind spots by turning sponsor activation into a collaborative labeling problem that fixes AI accuracy.

product_concept.name: CourtTruth: Real Share-of-Court Attribution
product_concept.first_screen: Split-screen showing a single court scene with two metrics: 'Raw Brand Count' (inflated numbers from net/venue branding) on the left vs. 'True Court Share' (corrected attribution) on the right. Toggle between view modes, with a dropdown to filter by the 7 affected brands (Head, Wilson, etc.).
product_concept.why_powerful: Sponsors pay premium rates for verified on-court presence; this product corrects systematic overcounting that's undervaluing sponsorship deals.

product_concept.name: JOOLAssify: Brand Disambiguation Engine
product_concept.first_screen: Video clip player showing a paddle/shoe/net brand detection with context labels: 'JOOLA (paddle)' vs 'JOOLA (shoes)' vs 'Other brand'. User validates which category the detected instance belongs to, with a progress bar showing '253 JOOLA instances disambiguated / 370 total clips processed.'
product_concept.why_powerful: JOOLA's dominant appearance (68% of clips) creates AI confusion that breaks sponsorship measurement for all competitors; disambiguation unlocks accurate portfolio analysis.
